title: Constexpr if statements

exercises.title: Platform-Dependent Size Message
exercises.prompt_md:
## Platform-Dependent Size Message

Write a program that uses `if constexpr` to print a message about the size of `int` on the current platform.

Rules:
- If `sizeof(int) == 4`: print `int is 4 bytes`
- Else if `sizeof(int) == 2`: print `int is 2 bytes`
- Else: print `int is an unusual size`

Then, on a second line, do the same for `double`:
- If `sizeof(double) == 8`: print `double is 8 bytes`
- Else: print `double is not 8 bytes`

This exercise practices using `sizeof` as a compile-time condition in `if constexpr`.

**Expected output on most platforms:**
```
int is 4 bytes
double is 8 bytes
```

**Note:** `sizeof` is always evaluated at compile time, making it a valid `if constexpr` condition.

test_cases.label: Standard platform sizes (int=4, double=8)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
int is 4 bytes
double is 8 bytes

exercises.title: Constexpr Configuration Selector
exercises.prompt_md:
## Constexpr Configuration Selector

Write a program that uses `if constexpr` to select between different configurations at compile time.

The program should:
1. Define a `constexpr int mode` set to the value read from the first line of the description below.
2. Use `if constexpr` to print the mode name and a corresponding action:
   - `mode == 1`: print `Mode: Debug` then `Verbose logging enabled`
   - `mode == 2`: print `Mode: Release` then `Optimizations enabled`
   - `mode == 3`: print `Mode: Test` then `Test harness active`
   - Any other value: print `Mode: Unknown` then `No special configuration`

Set `mode` to `2` in your solution.

**Expected output:**
```
Mode: Release
Optimizations enabled
```

**Note:** Because `mode` is `constexpr`, the compiler knows which branch to take at compile time. Only one branch will exist in the final binary.
